member.office_address: 1207 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3703

form_analysis.form_header_text: Congresswoman Maxine Dexter Fiscal Year 2027 Programmatic and Language Requests Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Congresswoman Dexter is thrilled to accept programmatic and language requests for FY27. The deadline to submit requests is 5:00pm PT on Friday, March 6. Should any questions arise while completing this form, please reach out to Congresswoman Dexter's Deputy Chief of Staff and Legislative Director, Grace Banfield, at Grace.Banfield@mail.house.gov.

sections.description: Information about the organization and individual submitting the request.

fields.label: Name of Requesting Organization
Footprint in Oregon's 3rd District (HQ presence, number of employees, constituent impact etc.)
Requesting Organization Point of Contact
Point of Contact Phone Number
Point of Contact Email
Are you requesting Congresswoman Dexter sign on to an existing appropriations letter or submit a new appropriations request?
fields.rationale: While an existing letter led by Reps. Bacon and Chu exists, programmatic requests to a specific member's office are typically submitted as new requests through their individual form to ensure it is tracked in their official priorities.

sections.description: Details for requesting the Congresswoman sign on to an existing appropriations letter.

fields.label: Is this a programmatic request, a language request, or both?
fields.rationale: The request context specifies this is a programmatic funding request for the Science Mission Directorate at a specific dollar amount ($9 billion) without accompanying report or bill language.

fields.label: Describe the request in three to five sentences.
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion programmatic request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) for FY 2027
* Provides a $1.75 billion (24%) increase over the FY 2026 enacted level to restore agency stability
* Funds high-priority missions as identified by the National Academies' peer-reviewed Decadal Surveys
* Maintains U.S. leadership in space exploration and discovery against expanding competition from China
fields.rationale: The request amount ($9B) and the specific increase over FY26 ($1.75B) are taken directly from the funding ask and topline history. Strategic arguments regarding Decadal Surveys and China competition are drawn from the advocacy talking points.

fields.label: Describe the impact of the request to constituents of Oregon's 3rd District in three to five sentences.
fields.draft_value:
* Supports local scientific innovation through recipients like Carbonplan, which has received $1,567,684 in NASA funding
* Leverages NASA's 3-to-1 economic return to stimulate Oregon's research and technology sectors
* Strengthens the local STEM talent pipeline by providing high-tech challenges and research opportunities
* Provides critical Earth science data used locally for weather forecasting and environmental management
fields.rationale: Cites the specific grant recipient (Carbonplan) and cumulative award amount ($1,567,684) found in the district spending data, combined with the 3-to-1 ROI talking point.

fields.label: Name of Member of Congress Leading Letter
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon and Judy Chu
fields.rationale: Identified as the leads for the house letter in the provided context.

fields.label: Name of Staff Member Coordinating Letter
fields.rationale: Staff member names for coordinating letters are not provided in the context.

fields.label: Letter Deadline
fields.rationale: The deadline for the letter is not specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Quill Link for Letter (if you do not have access to the Quill link, please write N/A)
fields.rationale: Quill links are internal congressional documents and not provided in this context.

fields.label: If submitting more than one request, please list the priority of this request.
fields.rationale: Internal priority ranking is determined by the submitter and cannot be known without their specific organizational preference.

fields.label: Describe the request in three to five sentences.
fields.draft_value: This request seeks $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to restore stability and American leadership in space science following recent fiscal disruptions. These funds will support critical missions across planetary science, astrophysics, and Earth science, ensuring the U.S. remains the global leader in exploration amidst rising international competition. By funding SMD at this level, Congress supports the decadal survey priorities and maintains a robust STEM workforce. This investment secures our nation's scientific future and keeps the U.S. at the forefront of discovery and innovation.
fields.rationale: Drafted using advocacy talking points and the specific $9B funding ask to provide a compelling programmatic justification.

fields.label: Describe the impact of the request to Oregon's 3rd District in three to five sentences.
fields.draft_value: NASA Science funding supports high-tech research and innovation in Oregon's 3rd District, as evidenced by Carbonplan receiving over $1.5 million in recent grant funding. These investments fuel the local knowledge economy and support specialized jobs that contribute to national climate and environmental research goals. By supporting NASA Science, the district directly participates in the global effort to understand our planet and the universe. Sustained funding ensures that local institutions can continue to provide cutting-edge research and inspire the next generation of Oregon's STEM professionals.
fields.rationale: Connects the specific grant recipient (Carbonplan) and funding amount ($1,567,684) found in the district context to the broader economic and scientific benefits.

fields.label: List all other offices to which you have submitted this request.
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Provide the name of any organization that might be opposed to this request and why.
fields.draft_value:
* No known organizations are opposed to this funding request
* The request is supported by major scientific bodies including the American Astronomical Society and American Geophysical Union
* Funding aligns with the non-partisan, peer-reviewed consensus of the National Academies
* Bipartisan support for restoring NASA Science funding was demonstrated in the FY 2026 appropriations cycle
fields.rationale: The reference values explicitly state that there are no known opposed organizations and list the AAS and AGU as allied organizations.

fields.label: If submitting more than one request, please list the priority of this request.
fields.rationale: Internal priority ranking is determined by the submitter and cannot be known without their specific organizational preference.
